reviewedFixFeatures.summary: Adds a real nested bash -> agent -> AgentBridge -> ScoopContext path and a real aborted-stream path, but the VAL-NEST-015 counter proof still is not attached to the actual cone-tool execution surface.
Makes partial pre-rejection execution observable by putting an allow-listed write before the disallowed segment and asserting the file bytes stay unchanged.
Captures the missing baseline full-workspace `npm run build` evidence for VAL-CI-010 and records it durably in committed validation artifacts.

Correctly replaces the unknown-model proxy with the real missing-provider path inside `ScoopContext.init()`, but the evidence still shows user-visible final stderr collapsing to `Agent not initialized`.

Attempts to preserve the specific init-time error, but only covers a mocked prompt-throw path; the real `ScoopContext` missing-provider flow still overwrites the earlier specific error with the later generic one.

remainingBlockers: VAL-NEST-015 proof is still detached from the real cone-only scoop-management tool execution surface; the current counter array stays zero regardless of the runtime path under test.

The fresh/no-provider path is now exercised and evidenced, but the user-facing `agent` stderr on the real path still collapses to `Agent not initialized` instead of a provider-identifying initialization error, so VAL-LIVE-010 remains unmet at the terminal surface.

blockingIssues.description: The round-2 remediation still does not prove VAL-NEST-015 on the real runtime path: its cone-tool counters are attached to a standalone `createScoopManagementTools(...)` array rather than the actual cone/tool execution surface used by the nested agent run.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Instrument the real cone-side scoop-management tool registration/execution surface that a nested-agent regression would actually hit, then rerun the real-context nesting validation.

blockingIssues.description: The fresh-profile remediation now proves the correct missing-provider path inside `ScoopContext.init()`, but the user-visible final stderr is still generic because the real `ScoopContext` path emits a later `onError("Agent not initialized")` that overwrites the earlier specific init error; the follow-up code fix only covered a mocked prompt-throw path and did not repair the real flow.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Preserve the first/specific init-time error on the real AgentBridge callback path (or suppress the generic follow-up overwrite), add a real-path regression test that matches the live missing-provider behavior, then rerun the fresh/no-provider VAL-LIVE-010 evidence capture.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Split validation-only remediation guidance from the normal code-change/TDD workflow so workers know when a task is evidence-only and should not be forced into tests-first/code-edit steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The passing `fix-hardening-rerun-baseline-full-build` review noted that `webapp-worker` reads like a pure code-change skill even though that remediation only updated committed validation artifacts and handoff evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add the xterm keyboard-input fallback and fresh/no-provider bootstrap procedure from `.factory/library/user-testing.md` to the live-validation instructions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-hardening-fresh-profile-val-live-010` review found the worker had to deviate manually because the skill still lacks the newly documented xterm-input fallback and fresh-profile bootstrap guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update this mission's feature metadata to prescribe the correct root cause/fix shape for the missing-provider error propagation bug.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as mission-specific guidance rather than durable shared-state knowledge; the actionable problem is already captured directly in the blocking review for `fix-agent-bridge-preserve-specific-init-error`.
